world.title: Madagascar baobab trees reveal 700 years of climate secrets and rainfall patterns
world.description: Rest of World News: The majestic baobabs of Madagascar are more than just awe-inspiring silhouettes on the horizon; they are the historical keepers of the Earth’s climate.

world.title: Nigeria charges 6 with treason over alleged coup plot
world.description: Nigerian authorities say they have charged six people with terrorism and treason over an alleged plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu, including a retired major general and a serving police inspector, according to charge sheet seen by The Associated Press on Tuesday.

world.title: Vietnam: Pre-Arrival Declaration Now Required in Ho Chi Minh City
world.description: Foreign nationals arriving at Tan Son Nhat International Airport in Ho Chi Minh City must now complete an online declaration form within three days prior to arrival. Delays in immigration processing are expected through the end of April 2026 as authorities and travelers adjust to the new system. This pilot program is positioned as a means to streamline clearance, but it also reflects an increased…

world.title: The Gray Tide: Latin America’s Demographic Transformation
world.description: A perfect storm of plunging birth rates and rapidly aging populations will break over Latin America in coming decades. Is there a silver lining?

science.title: Leonardo da Vinci Drew This Part of the Human Heart 500 Years Ago, and Scientists Just Solved the Mystery He Left Behind
science.description: After 500 years, scientists have finally decoded a heart structure first sketched by Leonardo da Vinci, linking it to cardiovascular disease risk.

science.title: Astronauts' brains retain memory of gravity even after months in space, study finds
science.description: Researchers working with 11 astronauts discovered that even in weightlessness, the brain continues to behave as if gravity is still present - a finding that could impact future space exploration.

science.title: Chinese astronomers discover pulsar that should not exist
science.description: Chinese astronomers have discovered a millisecond pulsar with an elevated spin-down rate, intense magnetic field and high energy loss. Such a pulsar should not form in the first place according to conventional theories.

science.title: Astronomers may have finally found the Universe’s first stars
science.description: Possible evidence of the universe's first stars has been discovered thanks to the detection of an extreme helium signal.

science.title: Curiosity Rover Uncovers New Organic Molecules on Mars
science.description: NASA`s Curiosity rover has detected over 20 organic molecules on Mars, providing major clues about the planet`s potential to support life billions of years ago.

science.title: Scientists Uncovered a Genetic Fusion Between Two Ancient Worlds in a 4,500-Year-Old Skeleton
science.description: Eighty percent North African. Twenty percent Fertile Crescent. One skeleton is forcing a rethink of migration in the age of the pyramids.

ai.title: Persistent Systems Reports Strong Growth Fueled by AI Strategy
ai.description: Persistent Systems reported a 33.73% increase in net profit for Q4 FY26, attributing growth to an AI-driven strategy. Revenue rose by 25%, with the order booking hitting USD 600.8 million. The company's workforce stands at over 27,500, with significant growth noted in India and global markets.
